metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `_ticket_graph_blob` injection mechanism for G7 with the correct AGENT-tier context path: inject `parent_plan` into G7's system prompt or initial user message (e.g., via the runner's prompt assembly for AGENT criteria), not via `_ticket_graph_blob` which is ISF-only.
findings.finding: The plan states the parent_plan context will be 'surfaced into the G7 criterion's context via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob`', but `_ticket_graph_blob` is only called for the ISF criterion (pass1.py:488 inside the ISF block). AGENT-tier criteria (G7 is `exec: AGENT`) do not receive pre-assembled context from `_ticket_graph_blob` — they get context through their agentic prompt/system message. The existing AGENT criteria (E4, G1G2, A1, G6) read the codebase directly via tool calls; they are not fed pre-assembled graph blobs.
findings.location: What section (CONTEXT ASSEMBLY) / pass1.py:281-302, pass1.py:488
findings.suggested_fix: Describe how the parent_plan field on PlanContext flows into the AGENT criterion's prompt, analogous to how the plan text is included in the agentic system prompt. The _ticket_graph_blob approach is only appropriate for single-turn (ISF) criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `PlanContext.has_parent_id` (a new bool property on det_floor.py:PlanContext reading `bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))`) to the Scope's explicit edit targets, alongside the existing `parent_plan` field addition.
findings.finding: The plan adds a `parent_plan` field to `PlanContext` (for context assembly) but `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py:337) needs to pass a `has_parent_id` boolean into `registry.applies()` to gate G7. `PlanContext` has no `has_parent_id` property today; the parent_id is buried in `ctx.state.get('parent_id')`. The plan describes adding `require_parent_id` to `applies()` and threading `has_parent_id` through `route_criteria()` from the `PlanContext`, but neither a `has_parent_id` property on `PlanContext` nor the `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` read in `route_criteria` is named as an edit target in the Scope.
findings.location: Scope section / det_floor.py:PlanContext / orchestrator.py:route_criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `has_parent_id` property to PlanContext analogous to `has_children` — `@property def has_parent_id(self) -> bool: return bool(self.state.get('parent_id'))` — and list it explicitly in Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `parent_plan` from `_ticket_graph_blob` (or directly from `ctx.parent_plan`) is threaded into the G7 agent call via the `extra_context` parameter in `run_pass1`, following the G5 decomposition-block pattern (inject only for chunks containing G7).
findings.finding: The plan names `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` as the source for surfacing `parent_plan` into G7's context, but does not specify that the threading to the G7 agent call goes through the `extra_context` parameter of `run_pass1`/`pass1_chunk` — the same seam the G5 decomposition block uses. An implementer reading only the plan text would know WHERE the data originates but not HOW it reaches the agent call, leaving a real assembly gap.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY' paragraph: 'surface it into the G7 criterion's context via pass1._ticket_graph_blob alongside the parent-ID string it already emits'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope or What section, add: 'In `run_pass1`, populate `extra_context` for the G7-bearing agent chunk from `_ticket_graph_blob(ctx)` (which now includes the `parent_plan` block), mirroring the G5 decomposition-state injection pattern (`extra = decomp_context if any(c.get("id") == "G5" ...) else ""`).'

findings.finding: The plan says `parent_plan` is surfaced into G7's context 'via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281)'. However, `_ticket_graph_blob` is called ONLY from `run_pass1` for the ISF criterion (pass1.py line 489), feeding `pass1_isf` — not the agent-criteria path. G7 runs via the `_chunk([crit], True)` path (pass1.py line 449), which goes through `_pass1_with_ladder` → `passes.pass1_chunk` using `extra_context`. If parent_plan must be pre-injected (not fetched by the agent's own `show_ticket` tool), the injection point is `extra_context`, not `_ticket_graph_blob`.
findings.location: Context Assembly section — 'surface it into the G7 criterion's context via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281)'; pass1.py lines 281-302 and 489
findings.suggested_fix: If pre-injection is intended: add parent_plan to `extra_context` in `run_pass1` for G7's agent chunk specifically (mirroring how `decomp_context` is injected only for G5-bearing chunks, pass1.py line 418). If agent self-fetch is intended: document this in the plan and remove `_ticket_graph_blob` as the surfacing point for G7.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the actual mechanism for injecting parent plan context into G7: since G7 is AGENT-tier, confirm whether it should read the parent ticket via a store-read tool call (consistent with how G1G2/E4/A1/G6 access codebase context) rather than reusing the ISF single-turn blob injection path.
findings.finding: The plan proposes surfacing `parent_plan` into the G7 criterion context 'via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob`'. `_ticket_graph_blob` is a single-turn ISF context helper (pass1.py:281-302, used only at pass1.py:488 for ISF). G7 is AGENT-tier; all existing AGENT-tier criteria (G1G2, E4, A1, G6) receive context through their agentic tool loop, not via pre-injected single-turn blobs. Reusing the ISF blob mechanism for an AGENT criterion is a category mismatch — a cargo-cult of the ISF single-turn pattern applied to a different execution tier.
findings.location: What section / pass1.py `_ticket_graph_blob` (line 281) / pass1.py ISF call (line 488)
findings.suggested_fix: For an AGENT criterion, the standard pattern is for the agent to call `show_ticket(parent_id)` via its tool loop. Inject `parent_id` into the G7 finder prompt (already available from `ctx.state.get('parent_id')`), and let the agent fetch the parent plan as a tool call — no `_ticket_graph_blob` modification needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test asserting that a parent description exceeding the char cap is truncated to at most the cap length in the assembled `parent_plan` field.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a bounded char-cap truncation for parent/grandparent plan text but provides no test asserting the truncation boundary — the test suite can go green with truncation logic that is silently broken (e.g., off-by-one, no-op), allowing oversized context to reach the LLM or empty context to reach the criterion.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / AC4 — 'a test asserting the assembly'
findings.suggested_fix: Include a fixture with a synthetic parent description longer than the cap and assert `len(context.parent_plan) <= CHAR_CAP` (or equivalent) in the context-assembly test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (`_validate_routing_entry`) to the Scope section as a named edit target, and extend it to recognise (and validate the boolean type of) the new `require_parent_id` applies_at key so the CI parity gate actually enforces the new axis.
findings.finding: The plan states 'validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis,' but the applies_at sub-key validator (_validate_routing_entry in src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131-212) is NOT listed as a named edit target in the Scope section. Currently, _validate_routing_entry only validates scope values and rejects two legacy keys; any unknown applies_at key (including require_parent_id) is silently ignored rather than validated. Without updating overlay.py, the new axis would pass validation undetected, making the Scope-listed 'validate_packaged_routing()+applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis' claim incomplete.
findings.location: Scope section / What section ('validate_packaged_routing() and the applies_at schema validator must accept the new axis')
findings.suggested_fix: In overlay.py's _validate_routing_entry, after validating `scope`, add: `if 'require_parent_id' in ap and not isinstance(ap['require_parent_id'], bool): raise CriteriaError(...)`. Also add it to the Scope section alongside registry.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` (`_validate_routing_entry`) to the Scope as the named applies_at schema validator edit target, and specify whether it must positively accept `require_parent_id` or merely not reject it.
findings.finding: The plan names 'the applies_at schema validator' as a required edit target for accepting the new `require_parent_id` axis, but does not identify the concrete file: `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry` (lines 131-212) is the actual applies_at validator. This function is re-exported into registry.py (`_validate_routing_entry = _overlay_core._validate_routing_entry`, registry.py:188) and called by both `validate_packaged_routing` and `effective_routing`. The Scope section omits `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` entirely.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py:131-212
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope section. In `_validate_routing_entry`, add a note (or active check) that `require_parent_id` is a valid boolean axis, mirroring how `scope` is validated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the numeric char cap (e.g., 4000 chars per ancestor level) for the `parent_plan` field truncation in the What/Scope/AC sections so the assembly test can pin it.
findings.finding: The `parent_plan` field's char-cap (the truncation bound applied to parent + grandparent title/description before injection) is referenced throughout the plan as 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap)' but the actual cap value is never specified anywhere in the plan. An executor implementing `PlanContext.parent_plan` population must invent the number.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify who is responsible for the store read: if the orchestrator pre-loads parent text into PlanContext (the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY approach), remove the 'it must read the parent ticket from the store' description from the criterion registration bullet and instead say the criterion *receives* pre-loaded parent context; or, if the AGENT criterion fetches at runtime, remove the orchestrator-side assembly description and adjust the PlanContext/pass1 mechanism accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan contradicts itself about who reads the parent ticket from the store: the criterion registration says `exec: AGENT` and 'it must read the parent ticket from the store, like the other codebase-grounded criteria,' implying the criterion agent fetches the parent at runtime; but the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section says the orchestrator's `_assemble_context_uncached` reads the parent chain from the store and pre-loads it into `PlanContext.parent_plan`, implying the orchestrator does the fetch. An implementer following the first description would build store-access into the criterion prompt/agent; one following the second would build it into the orchestrator — these are different code paths and the plan does not reconcile them.
findings.location: What section, first bullet (criterion registration) vs. What/CONTEXT ASSEMBLY sub-section
findings.suggested_fix: Since the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section is more detailed and architecturally consistent with the 'additive, existing criteria unaffected' principle, prefer the orchestrator-pre-load approach: change the registration description to 'exec: AGENT (receives pre-loaded parent context from PlanContext.parent_plan, assembled by the orchestrator)' and remove the implication that the criterion agent reads the store directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the move ID key (e.g., '13'), the template text, and the `applies_when` value (e.g., `['G7']` or absent for universal) for the new parent-realign coach move in the Scope or What section.
findings.finding: The new parent-realign coach move is listed as a Scope deliverable ('the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)') but neither its move ID key nor its `applies_when` field is defined. Existing moves use string integer keys ('1'–'12'); `applies_when` controls which criterion findings can trigger a move. The plan is silent on both, leaving the executor to invent the key and decide whether the move is universally applicable or scoped to G7.
findings.location: Scope — 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)'

findings.finding: The plan introduces content truncation ('truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps') into PlanContext, directly contradicting the explicit 'never truncated, never content-chunked, by design' invariant in both `PlanContext`'s docstring (det_floor.py:96-98) and the orchestrator's module docstring (orchestrator.py:8). The char cap value is unspecified, creating new unnamed config surface.
findings.location: What section: 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)' and Scope section: 'det_floor.py PlanContext (add parent_plan field)'

findings.finding: The new `parent_plan` field on `PlanContext` is described as 'bounded (truncate each to a char cap)' — but `PlanContext`'s docstring explicitly states 'content is ALWAYS whole (no truncation, no content-chunking, by design)'. This is a semantic contract violation in the plan that should be resolved: either the `parent_plan` field lives outside `PlanContext` (passed separately to G7's context injection) and the dataclass docstring stays true, or the docstring must be updated to carve out the `parent_plan` field. The plan does not acknowledge this contradiction.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY; det_floor.py `PlanContext` docstring (line 96–98)
findings.suggested_fix: Pass the bounded parent plan text as `extra_context` to `pass1_chunk` (the existing mechanism already used for G5's decomposition state block), bypassing the need to add a truncated field to `PlanContext` at all. This keeps `PlanContext` whole-content, reuses the exact injection seam, and is aligned with how `decomposition_state_block` already works.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a concrete default char cap for the parent_plan injection (e.g., 'truncate parent description to 4 000 chars, grandparent to 2 000 chars') in the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section so the executor does not need to choose an arbitrary value.
findings.finding: The char cap for truncating parent/grandparent plan text injected into `parent_plan` is described as 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap)' but the actual limit is never stated. The executor must choose an appropriate value — a defaultable gap that should be a stated decision in the plan.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)': 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph: 'Cap: parent title+description ≤ 4 000 chars, grandparent title+description ≤ 2 000 chars; truncate at the nearest sentence boundary or hard-cut with a […truncated] marker.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add error handling and graceful-degradation spec for the parent-chain store read in `_assemble_context_uncached`: define the fallback behavior (e.g., skip G7 with a logged warning if parent plan text is unavailable), add an observability hook (log/metric) on failure, and cover the empty-parent-plan case in the G7 rubric or routing guard.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new store read (parent chain lookup in `_assemble_context_uncached`) and a new AGENT-tier LLM call (G7 criterion), but specifies no error handling, timeout, or graceful-degradation path for either. If the store read fails (parent not found, store unavailable, timeout) or returns empty/absent plan text, the plan is silent on whether G7 skips, logs and continues, or propagates an exception that could impact the broader review pipeline.
findings.location: What section — context-assembly path; orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached`; AC: 'The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context'
findings.suggested_fix: In `_assemble_context_uncached`, wrap the parent-chain store read in a try/except; on failure, set `parent_plan = None` and log a warning with the ticket ID. In `applies()` or the G7 context injector, treat `parent_plan is None` as a routing skip (G7 does not fire if parent plan is unreadable), and emit a metric counter `plan_review.g7.parent_context_unavailable`. Add this degradation behavior to the Scope and an AC: 'If the parent ticket is unavailable, G7 is skipped and the skip is logged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior for a failed parent ticket read in `_assemble_context_uncached` (e.g., `parent_plan = ''`, log a warning, G7 receives empty context and ABSTAINS), analogous to the existing children-read fallback pattern.
findings.finding: The plan adds parent-plan text injection via `_assemble_context_uncached` reading the parent chain from the store, but specifies no fallback behavior when the parent ticket read fails (e.g., store I/O error, orphaned ticket with a stale `parent_id`). The existing children-read path has an explicit warning+continue pattern (orchestrator.py:175-177), but no analogous pattern is specified for the new parent read, leaving G7 in an undefined state when parent context is unavailable.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY' / orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached`
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the children pattern: wrap the parent `show_ticket` call in a try/except, log a warning on failure, and set `parent_plan = ''` (empty string). G7's rubric should then specify: if `parent_plan` is empty, ABSTAIN with a coverage note rather than fabricating findings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the parent/grandparent plan text char-cap as a named constant (e.g., `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP` in a plan-review config or det_floor.py) rather than an inline magic number, so it is discoverable and tunable without a code search.
findings.finding: The char-cap used to truncate parent and grandparent plan text in `parent_plan` context assembly is described only as 'a char cap' with no specified value, named constant, or config key. This threshold governs a cost/quality tradeoff (too small = G7 misses parent scope; too large = token budget blown) that is very likely to need tuning once real tickets are reviewed, but the plan does not make it configurable or even name where it will live.
findings.location: What section — context assembly; Scope section — det_floor.py PlanContext, orchestrator.py _assemble_context_uncached
findings.suggested_fix: Define `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP: int = <N>` in det_floor.py alongside PlanContext (or in a plan_review config module), reference it in `_assemble_context_uncached`, and note the value in the G7 promotion-path doc so operators know what to adjust if false-fire rates correlate with truncation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a developer-facing note (ADR entry, CLAUDE.md section, or inline in the routing schema doc) capturing (a) the new `require_parent_id` applies_at axis as a supported routing contract and (b) the `parent_plan` PlanContext field as available criterion context, so future criterion authors can discover and reuse both.
findings.finding: The plan introduces two new architectural contracts — the `require_parent_id` boolean axis added to the shared `applies_at` schema (a new routing API that all future criterion authors must understand), and the `parent_plan` field injected into `det_floor.PlanContext` (a new pipeline stage that changes the context-assembly contract) — but specifies no ADR or developer-facing doc update (e.g., CLAUDE.md, a routing-schema guide) to capture these decisions. Future criterion authors consulting only the existing docs will not discover that `require_parent_id` is a valid routing axis or that `parent_plan` exists in context.
findings.location: Scope section (registry.py applies_at schema extension; det_floor.py PlanContext; orchestrator.py _assemble_context_uncached)
findings.suggested_fix: Append a short section to docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (already being regenerated) under a 'Routing axis reference' heading listing all supported applies_at keys including `require_parent_id`, and add a one-paragraph note about the `parent_plan` context field. Alternatively, add a CLAUDE.md entry under plan-review development conventions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test (and specify the fallback behavior) for `_assemble_context_uncached` when the parent ticket is not found in the store — e.g., `parent_plan` defaults to empty string/None and G7 is skipped or emits a warning rather than raising.
findings.finding: The context-assembly path (`_assemble_context_uncached` reading the parent chain from the store) has no specified behavior or test for the case where the parent_id is set on a leaf but the parent ticket is absent from the store (deleted, archived, or referential inconsistency). This is a new failure path introduced by the feature.
findings.location: Scope / AC5 — context assembly; orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `parent_plan` needs to be a field on `PlanContext` or can instead be threaded as a parameter only to G7's agent context assembly path (e.g., returned from `_assemble_context_uncached` as a side-product, or read lazily inside G7's agentic tool call) — avoid widening the shared struct if only G7 consumes it.
findings.finding: Adding a `parent_plan` field to `PlanContext` (a shared dataclass with 14+ construction sites and consumers across det_floor, pass1, orchestrator, attest, sizing, workflow_ops) for a single criterion's (G7) context need violates YAGNI. The parent plan text is only consumed by G7; all other criteria are explicitly documented as unaffected. `PlanContext` already carries `state: dict` containing `parent_id`, and the parent text could be threaded as a local variable in `_assemble_context_uncached` without widening the shared struct.
findings.location: What section / det_floor.py `PlanContext` (line 95) / orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached` (line 163)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `workflow_ops.py` (the `plan_review_grounding` step description and inline comment listing CODEBASE_GROUNDED members) and `attest.py` to the Scope as consumers requiring comment/documentation updates when G7 is added to `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`.
findings.finding: The plan says G7 reads the parent ticket from the store 'like the other codebase-grounded criteria' and lists adding G7 to `CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED` in the What section, but the Scope section's file list omits `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` (registry.py:66) and two of its consumers outside plan_review/: `workflow_ops.py:plan_review_grounding` (which decides whether the AGENTIC verifier fires, based on whether any finding cites a CODEBASE_GROUNDED criterion) and `attest.py:76` (which records grounded coverage). Adding G7 to CODEBASE_GROUNDED without updating these consumers' documentation strings is an unenumerated change impact.
findings.location: Scope section / registry.py:66 / workflow_ops.py:207-232 / attest.py:76

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` to the Scope and specify the `_validate_routing_entry` change needed to accept (and optionally validate) the new `require_parent_id` axis in `applies_at`.
findings.finding: The `require_parent_id` axis added to `applies_at` in `criteria_routing.json` must also be accepted (and optionally validated) by `_validate_routing_entry` in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py`, but that file is absent from the plan's Scope. Currently `_validate_routing_entry` only explicitly validates `scope`, `suppress_types`, `suppress_when`, and rejects legacy keys — an unrecognized key in `applies_at` is silently ignored, so G7's routing entry will pass the parity gate without the new axis actually being enforced or validated.
findings.location: Scope / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py — 'add the `require_parent_id` axis to the applies_at schema + `applies()`'
findings.suggested_fix: In `overlay.py:_validate_routing_entry`, after the `scope` validation block, add a check: `require_parent_id = ap.get('require_parent_id'); if require_parent_id is not None and not isinstance(require_parent_id, bool): raise CriteriaError(...)`. Add `overlay.py` to the plan's Scope.

findings.finding: The `applies()` function in registry.py (lines 311-339) has no mechanism for a `has_parent_id` condition — it supports `scope` (container vs leaf), `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when` only. The plan gates G7 on '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)' via `applies_at`, but `scope: ["leaf"]` alone only gates on `not has_children`, not on `has parent_id`. A parentless leaf (a top-level task with no children) would also satisfy `scope: ["leaf"]` and would incorrectly receive G7. The plan names `applies_at` as the mechanism but does not name `applies()` as an edit target requiring extension to carry a `has_parent_id` dimension.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets: applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)'; registry.py `applies()` lines 311-339
findings.suggested_fix: Add `has_parent_id: bool = False` to `applies()`'s signature and a corresponding `require_parent` key to the `applies_at` schema. Update `criteria_routing.json` G7 entry with `"require_parent": true`. Name `applies()` explicitly as an edit target in the Scope section.

findings.finding: The plan asserts `applies_at` can gate G7 on `(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)`, but the existing routing schema and `applies()` function have no mechanism for a `parent_id`-presence predicate — `scope` only supports `[container|leaf]` and `suppress_when` only has `test_task`/`mechanical_leaf` conditions; the `_validate_routing_entry` validator enforces this closed vocabulary and would silently ignore or reject an unrecognized `applies_at` key.
findings.location: What / ROUTING section; Scope (registry.py `applies_at` gate); AC line 1 (routing test)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `suppress_when: ["parentless_leaf"]` condition to G7's `applies_at` entry, implement `is_parentless_leaf(plan, ctx)` in registry.py (returns True when `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` is falsy), and extend `applies()` to check it. This mirrors the existing `mechanical_leaf`/`test_task` suppress_when pattern and requires no schema validation changes beyond adding the new condition name to the allowed vocabulary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or explicitly defer with rationale to a named child) that exercises G7 end-to-end against a real ticket-pair (parent + leaf) using a live LLM call, asserting either a clean result or a finding in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar — satisfying E6(f) for the new AGENT code path.
findings.finding: G7 is a new `exec: AGENT` criterion that makes live LLM calls in production, but no acceptance criterion exercises the criterion end-to-end against a real ticket and a real LLM. All ACs are fixture-driven, routing-level, or file-existence checks. The new code path — the agent reading the parent from the store, assembling context, and calling the LLM — is never exercised as it will run in production.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all seven ACs; What section 'exec: AGENT'
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'An integration smoke test (skipped in offline CI, run in a designated live-LLM test environment) invokes G7 on a fixture ticket-pair via the real store + real LLM and asserts the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar contains a valid G7 entry (criteria_id=G7, posture=advisory). Alternatively, explicitly defer this to the advisory observation window (72b6) with a documented rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a proving command within an existing AC) that verifies the guide is regenerated and reflects G7 — e.g., 'make docs && git diff --exit-code docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md exits 0' or 'the guide contains a G7 entry confirmed by grep -c G7 docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md returning ≥ 1'.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists 'the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md' as an edit target, but no acceptance criterion verifies that this file was updated correctly (or at all). It is possible to satisfy all seven ACs while shipping a stale or missing guide.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md'; Acceptance Criteria — no corresponding AC
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC6 or add a new AC: 'make docs (or the project's equivalent regeneration command) exits 0 and docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md contains a G7 entry (grep -E "\bG7\b" docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md returns ≥ 1 match).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion covering the regenerated `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` — e.g., 'docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md is regenerated and includes a G7 entry describing its routing, semantics, posture, and parent-wins rule.'
findings.finding: The Scope section lists 'the regenerated docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md' as a deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — there is no AC requiring the guide to exist, be updated, or be CI-verifiable. It is an orphan deliverable.
findings.location: Scope section (last paragraph) and Acceptance Criteria section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the new `parent_plan` field on `PlanContext` is optional with a default (e.g. `parent_plan: Optional[str] = None`) and confirm existing construction sites require no changes, or enumerate the construction sites that must be updated.
findings.finding: PlanContext is extended with a new `parent_plan` field, but the plan does not specify that this field is optional/defaulted (e.g., `Optional[str] = None`). If PlanContext is a dataclass with required positional fields, every existing construction site in the codebase breaks silently — yet the plan only claims existing criteria that don't *consume* `parent_plan` are unaffected, not that existing *constructors* are unaffected.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)' / det_floor.py PlanContext field addition
findings.suggested_fix: Declare the field as `parent_plan: Optional[str] = None` in the dataclass, ensuring backward-compatible construction everywhere.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `require_parent_id` schema axis with a direct inline guard in `route_criteria()` (analogous to the ISF `if cid == 'ISF': continue` pattern), reading `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` — eliminate the schema extension and its four cascading touch-points unless a second criterion immediately needs the same axis.
findings.finding: The `require_parent_id` routing axis added to `applies_at` and `applies()` is a premature abstraction that fails the Rule-of-Three: it will have exactly one call-site (G7) and zero existing uses. The existing ISF special-case pattern (a direct `if cid == 'ISF': continue` guard in `route_criteria`) already handles single-criterion routing exceptions without schema proliferation.
findings.location: What section / registry.py `applies()` (lines 311-339) / orchestrator.py `route_criteria()` (lines 337-375)
findings.suggested_fix: In `route_criteria`, after the ISF guard, add: `if cid == 'G7' and not ctx.state.get('parent_id'): continue`. No schema change, no `applies()` change, no validator change. If the axis is needed by 3+ criteria in the near term, promote it to `applies_at` then.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add one sentence of positive rationale for the `require_parent_id` routing-axis choice over a rubric-level self-gate, e.g. 'a routing axis prevents wasted AGENT calls for parentless tickets; a rubric self-gate would still launch and pay for the agent loop before returning not-applicable.'
findings.finding: The plan provides a positive rationale for the claim-time AGENT approach overall, but does not state why `require_parent_id` is implemented as a new routing-axis on `applies_at` rather than as a rubric-level self-gate inside G7's prompt (the G5 GATE-check pattern, where the rubric itself says 'if condition not met → PASS not-applicable'). Both are viable; the routing-axis approach couples the new boolean to the schema and `_validate_routing_entry`, while the rubric self-gate requires no schema change. The absence of rationale for this design choice leaves the implementer without guidance if the schema-extension path hits friction.
findings.location: What section — ROUTING / MECHANISM paragraph describing `require_parent_id` as a new `applies_at` axis

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the `require_parent_id` schema axis as the preferred routing mechanism over alternatives (a new `suppress_when` condition computed from ticket metadata, or an inline guard in `route_criteria()`), citing why the schema extension is warranted.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `require_parent_id` as a new boolean axis on the `applies_at` schema without justifying this novel extension vs. established alternatives — specifically, whether a new `suppress_when` condition (already the mechanism for metadata-derived suppression like `test_task`/`mechanical_leaf`) or an inline guard in `route_criteria()` could satisfy the requirement without schema surgery.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING … add a new boolean axis `require_parent_id` to the `applies_at` schema'; registry.py:311-339
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence rationale in the What section: e.g., '`suppress_when` conditions are computed from plan text only — they cannot observe ticket-store metadata (parent_id). An inline guard in `route_criteria()` would work but would not be schema-declared or testable via `validate_packaged_routing()`. The `require_parent_id` axis makes the gate declarative and uniformly validated — hence the schema extension.' If this rationale doesn't hold, prefer the inline guard.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve whether G7's `parent_plan` context injection is necessary given that AGENT-tier criteria already call `show_ticket()` live; if the injection is retained (e.g., for bounded truncation or deterministic context), state the justification explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan builds a `parent_plan` context-injection mechanism in `PlanContext` and `_assemble_context_uncached` for G7 (an AGENT-tier criterion), but does not resolve whether this is necessary given that AGENT-tier criteria (like G3/G4) already call `show_ticket()` live themselves — the plan asserts the injection is required without explaining why the agent cannot simply fetch the parent plan via its own tool call.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)… add a `parent_plan` field… to `det_floor.PlanContext`'; Scope section — 'the context-assembly path for the NEW parent-plan injection'

findings.finding: The plan states G7's `applies_at` gates on '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)', but the existing `applies()` function in registry.py only receives `has_children`, `ticket_type`, and `plan` — it has no way to evaluate `has parent_id`. The `applies_at` schema currently supports `scope`, `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when`; none of these can express 'has a parent_id'. The plan does not specify how this new routing condition is wired into the existing machinery.
findings.location: Scope section: 'registry.py (register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED; applies_at leaf gate)'; What section: 'applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `require_parent_id: true` field to G7's `applies_at` routing entry, extend `applies()` to accept an optional `parent_id: str | None` parameter, and pass `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` from `route_criteria`. This is the minimal, schema-consistent extension.

findings.finding: The `applies_at` schema and `registry.applies()` have no `has_parent_id` axis, so the plan's required routing gate ('ticket has parent_id AND no children') cannot be expressed declaratively today. The plan hedges this as 'if not expressible declaratively → registry.py' but does not commit to a design: (a) add a new `requires_parent` key to the `applies_at` schema and extend `applies()`, (b) add an imperative branch in `route_criteria()` (precedent: ISF special-case), or (c) rely on the AGENT rubric to self-gate via PASS not-applicable for parentless leaves. Options (a) and (b) require extending the routing machinery; option (c) would silently run the criterion on ALL leaves (incurring AGENT-tier cost) and rely on the model to self-suppress — a different posture than the plan's AC ('a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets'). The AC explicitly demands a routing-level skip, which rules out (c), making the required extension un-specified.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (applies_at leaf gate if not expressible declaratively)'; AC1: 'a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `requires_parent: true` to the G7 `applies_at` block in criteria_routing.json, extend `registry.applies()` to accept `has_parent_id: bool = False` and check `if ap.get('requires_parent') and not has_parent_id: return False`, then thread `has_parent_id=bool(ctx.state.get('parent_id'))` through the three call sites (orchestrator.route_criteria, workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria, production_batch_runner). This is purely additive — existing criteria have no `requires_parent` key so the default (False → no constraint) is backward-compatible.

findings.finding: `route_criteria()` in orchestrator.py (lines 337-375) calls `registry.applies()` and would need to pass `has_parent_id` presence to honour G7's parent-gating — but it is not named as an edit target. Similarly, `workflow_ops.py` (line 151, which calls `route_criteria`) and `production_batch_runner.py` are unenumerated consumers of the routing path that would need to propagate `has_parent_id` from `ctx.state`.
findings.location: Scope section — names orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached` but not `route_criteria`; orchestrator.py lines 337-375; workflow_ops.py line 151

findings.finding: The `applies()` function in registry.py has no `require_parent_id` axis — it gates on `scope` (container/leaf), `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when`, but cannot express 'only when a parent_id is present'. The plan says G7 applies when '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)', but the second condition (has parent_id) is not expressible in the current routing schema, so the described leaf gate cannot be implemented without extending `applies()` or adding a new routing key. The plan does not name how this extension is accomplished.
findings.location: What section — routing, applies_at gate; registry.py `applies()` function (lines 311–339)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `require_parent_id: true` to the `applies_at` schema and extend `registry.applies()` to check `ctx.state.get('parent_id') is not None` (passing `ctx` or the state dict as an argument) when that key is present. Alternatively, add a `suppress_when: no_parent_id` condition with a matching predicate in `applies()`.

findings.finding: The `applies_at` gate the plan specifies for G7 requires a dual condition — `(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)` — but the existing `applies()` function in registry.py accepts only `has_children`, `ticket_type`, and `plan`; the `applies_at` routing schema has no `require_parent_id` (or equivalent) key. Adding `"scope": ["leaf"]` to the G7 routing entry handles the no-children half but leaves the has-parent_id condition with no specified enforcement point — not in `applies_at` schema, not in `applies()`, and not described as rubric-internal logic.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets: applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)' / registry.py applies() line 311
findings.suggested_fix: The lowest-blast option: in the G7 rubric, add a GATE sentence — 'If the injected `parent_plan` context is absent or empty, emit PASS not-applicable immediately' — and document that `parent_plan` is populated only when the ticket has a parent_id (so an absent field IS the gate). This avoids changing the `applies()` signature or the routing schema.

findings.finding: AC4's 'in-session proof' is a merge-time comment on the ticket, which is not a repo artifact verifiable by CI and is a one-time transition action rather than a durable code-state. No proving command exists for this deliverable, making it manually inspectable only.
findings.location: AC4 — 'In-session proof: a comment on this ticket documents the promotion-decision PATH — the fire-rate/validity query, the 0.6–0.7 promotion band + trigger, and where the decision is recorded.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'commit `docs/g7-promotion-plan.md` containing (a) the REVIEW_RESULT fire-rate/validity query, (b) the 0.6–0.7 promotion trigger definition, and (c) the pointer to 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d.' Then update AC4 to: 'In-session proof: `docs/g7-promotion-plan.md` exists in the PR and contains all three promotion-path items (verified by a file-existence + content-anchor test or a CI lint rule).'

findings.finding: AC4's 'the promotion decision path is documented on this ticket' is a post-implementation editorial action with no in-session proving command or artifact spec — it cannot be verified at claim time and is not a durable code/repo end-state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'a `docs/decisions/g7-promotion-criteria.md` file documenting the field-data thresholds and window that will trigger the blocking promotion review.' Then the AC becomes: 'The promotion criteria file exists at docs/decisions/g7-promotion-criteria.md and states the observation window and fire-rate/adjudication threshold for promotion.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
